div.head: 75. Editorial Note
div.p: Following the meeting at 5 p.m. on the Austrian State Treaty (see Document 73), the Foreign Ministers of the United Kingdom, the Soviet Union, and France attended a dinner given by Secretary of State Dulles at Ambassador Thompson’s residence. In the course of their conversation during and after the dinner the Foreign Ministers discussed Formosa and a Four-Power Conference at some length and Indochina, disarmament, and the Berlin Autobahn tax briefly. For a report on the discussion of Formosa and a Four-Power Conference, see Document 116; memoranda of the other conversations are in Department of State, Conference Files: Lot 60 D 627, CF 445.
